reason: IMPERIUM sovereignty: external frameworks can inspire protocols but must not become the master architecture.
imperium_rule: No external framework is introduced by this harvest. Implementation must remain IMPERIUM-native until Owner explicitly approves a tool.

reason: Free multi-agent conversation can cascade hallucinations, duplicate work, or hide ownership.
imperium_rule: Use route cards, typed envelopes, organ ownership, and checkpoints instead of unconstrained chatter.

reason: Runtime outputs prove activity but are not architecture or law.
imperium_rule: Runtime goes to RUNS/RUNTIME layer, stays ignored or receipt-backed, and requires promotion gates.

reason: Stateful agents and teams can conflict when called concurrently.
imperium_rule: Stateful Organ-Agents are single-writer by default; parallelism requires concurrency admission.

reason: Tool execution without manifest, side-effect declaration, receipts, and permission gates creates hidden risk.
imperium_rule: Every tool/skill needs manifest, input/output schema, side-effect policy, receipts, and verification.
